seoTitle: How to Export a Power BI Report to PPT, PDF, or PBIX
metaDescription: Step-by-step guide to exporting a Power BI report to PPT, PDF, or PBIX format, so you can share insights in whatever format your audience needs.
intro: Different audiences call for different file formats, and Power BI supports exporting the same report to PowerPoint, PDF, or its native PBIX file depending on what's needed. This guide walks through each export option step by step. It's useful for business analysts and project managers who need flexibility in how they hand off reports to stakeholders.

steps.heading: Export to PPT
steps.body: Use the Export option in Power BI and choose PowerPoint to generate a slide-based version of your report, useful for presentations and meetings.

steps.heading: Export to PDF
steps.body: Select the PDF export option when you need a static, print-friendly document that preserves the exact layout of your report pages.

steps.heading: Export to PBIX
steps.body: Download the full PBIX file when you need to share the underlying report file itself, including its data model, for someone else to open in Power BI Desktop.

steps.heading: Explore use cases and examples
steps.body: Match the format to the purpose, such as PDF for a static handoff, PPT for a live presentation, and PBIX when a colleague needs to edit the report directly.

steps.heading: Consider automating exports
steps.body: For recurring reporting needs, look into automation tools that can trigger these exports on a schedule rather than doing it manually each time.

faq.question: Which export format keeps the report interactive?
faq.answer: None of these three formats preserve full interactivity; PBIX opens as an editable report in Power BI Desktop, but PPT and PDF are both static outputs.

faq.question: Can I export a report to PBIX if I only have viewer access?
faq.answer: Typically you need at least edit or build permissions on the report to download the PBIX file, since viewer-only access often restricts this option.

faq.question: Is PDF export good for printing a report?
faq.answer: Yes, PDF is generally the best choice when a report needs to be printed, since it preserves the exact visual layout at a fixed resolution.

faq.question: Do exported PPT or PDF files include every page of the report?
faq.answer: Most export options let you choose between exporting the current page only or the entire report, so you have control over what gets included.
